answer:
**Allama Iqbal's *Bang-E-Dara* poems (the only Iqbal works in the corpus) that explicitly name specific historical or religious figures include the following:**

- **Bang-E-Dara Part 1, ch. 24 ("Fikr-e-insaan par teri hasti se yeh roshan hua")**: Alludes strongly to the poet Mirza Ghalib (buried in Delhi) and compares him to Johann Wolfgang von Goethe (buried in Weimar), though their names are not stated outright.[^1]

- **Bang-E-Dara Part 1, ch. 26 ("Hindustani Bachon Ka Qaumi Geet")**: Names Moinuddin Chishti, Guru Nanak, Moses, and Prophet Noah.[^2]

- **Bang-E-Dara Part 2, ch. 8 ("Abdul Qadir Ke Naam")**: Dedicated to Abdul Qadir (likely a contemporary figure); also references literary-historical figures like Sa'di.[^3]

- **Bang-E-Dara Part 2, ch. 23 ("Swami Ram Tirath")**: Names Swami Ram Tirath (Rama Tirath, 1873–1906, Indian poet-philosopher) in the title and context.[^4]

- **Bang-E-Dara Part 3, ch. 11 ("Huzoor Risalat Ma'ab Mein")**: Names the Prophet Muhammad (referred to as the "Exalted Prophet").[^5]

- **Bang-E-Dara Part 3, ch. 17 ("Ram")**: Names Ram (Rama, presented as Hindustan's spiritual guide).[^6]

**No other Iqbal poems in the corpus name specific historical figures by name (though many allude to prophets, poets, or empires generally, e.g., *Goristan-e-Shahi* in Part 3, ch. 9, evokes Mughal kings without naming them).**[^7]

[^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 24, [translation/](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/24-fikr-e-insaan-par-teri-hasti-se-yeh-roshan-hua/translation/).

[^2]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 26, [p-da6786](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/26-hindustani-bachon-ka-qaumi-geet/translation/#p-da6786), [p-a9f72d](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/26-hindustani-bachon-ka-qaumi-geet/translation/#p-a9f72d).

[^3]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, ch. 8, [translation/](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/08-abdul-qadir-ke-naam/translation-2/).

[^4]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, ch. 23, [p-45d5d6](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/23-swami-ram-tirath/translation-2/#p-45d5d6).

[^5]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, ch. 11, [translation/](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/11-huzoor-risalat-maab-mein/translation/).

[^6]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, ch. 17, [translation/](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/17-ram/translation-2/).

[^7]: Scanned via wiki cards, chapter lists, targeted reads, and `search_corpus` across all three parts (~100 poems total); no additional explicit names found.
